title: Quiz: Derivative Fundamentals
description: Test your understanding of derivative instruments and markets

questions.question: What defines a derivative financial instrument?

options.text: It is traded only on exchanges
Its value is derived from an underlying asset

questions.explanation: A derivative is defined by the fact that its value is derived from (depends on) the value of an underlying asset, index, or reference rate such as stocks, bonds, commodities, or interest rates.

questions.question: What is the main difference between forwards and futures?

options.text: Futures cannot be used for hedging

questions.explanation: The main difference is that futures are standardized contracts traded on exchanges with daily mark-to-market, while forwards are customized OTC contracts settled at expiration.

questions.question: What is the purpose of daily mark-to-market in futures?

options.text: To determine the final delivery price

questions.explanation: Daily mark-to-market transfers profits and losses each day, preventing counterparty risk from accumulating. The clearinghouse ensures all positions are settled daily.

questions.question: To hedge a long stock position, which futures position should you take?

options.text: Either long or short depending on volatility

questions.explanation: To hedge a long stock position, you should short futures. If stocks fall, the short futures position gains, offsetting the stock losses. The hedge creates an opposite exposure.

questions.question: A forward contract has counterparty risk because there is no clearinghouse guarantee.
questions.explanation: True. Forwards are OTC contracts between two parties with direct counterparty exposure. Unlike futures, there is no exchange clearinghouse to guarantee performance.

questions.question: What does 'contango' mean in futures markets?

options.text: Spot price is higher than futures price
Futures price is higher than spot price

questions.explanation: Contango is when futures prices are higher than spot prices. This typically occurs when storage costs and interest rates exceed any convenience yield from holding the physical asset.

questions.question: What is the minimum variance hedge ratio used for?

options.text: Determining optimal position to minimize variance

questions.explanation: The minimum variance hedge ratio (h* = ρ × σ_asset/σ_hedge) determines the optimal number of hedge contracts to minimize the variance of the combined hedged position.

questions.question: Which are advantages of futures over forwards? (Select all that apply)

options.text: No counterparty risk due to clearinghouse

questions.explanation: Futures advantages include no counterparty risk (clearinghouse guarantee), higher liquidity for popular contracts, and transparent exchange pricing. Customizable terms is actually an advantage of forwards, not futures.
